---
title: ""
format:
  docx:
    reference-doc: resume-reference.docx
    toc: false
---

::: {custom-style="ResumeName"}
**Taylor Morris**
:::

::: {custom-style="ResumeContact"}
\(555\) 634-9182 | [taylor.morris@gmail.com](mailto:taylor.morris@gmail.com) | [linkedin.com/in/taylor-morris](https://www.linkedin.com/in/taylor-morris)
:::

# EDUCATION

| **Stanford University** | Stanford, CA |
| --- | ---: |
| *School of Engineering* | September 2019 -- June 2021 |
| *Master of Science, Management Science and Engineering* |  |

| **Ohio State University** | Columbus, OH |
| --- | ---: |
| *College of Arts and Sciences* | September 2014 -- May 2018 |
| *Bachelor of Science, Statistics; Minor in Computer Science* |  |

# PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

| **Veridian Technologies** | Austin, TX |
| --- | ---: |
| *Senior Strategy and Analytics Manager* | March 2023 -- Present |

- Lead a team of five analysts supporting go-to-market strategy and revenue operations for a $420M SaaS product line, owning quarterly business reviews and executive-facing reporting
- Built and maintain a real-time performance dashboard in Tableau consolidating pipeline, churn, and expansion data across three regional sales teams, reducing reporting prep time by 60%
- Partnered with Finance and Product to design a pricing sensitivity model that informed a tiered pricing restructure, contributing to a 14% increase in average contract value within two quarters
- Scoped and managed cross-functional initiatives across Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success to improve net revenue retention, increasing NRR from 108% to 117% year-over-year
- Mentored two junior analysts on SQL, data modeling, and stakeholder communication; both received promotions within 18 months

| **Veridian Technologies** | Austin, TX |
| --- | ---: |
| *Strategy and Analytics Analyst* | August 2021 -- March 2023 |

- Supported strategic planning cycles by building financial models and competitive landscape analyses used to prioritize $18M in annual product investment
- Developed and maintained SQL pipelines to automate recurring reporting on customer health, usage, and cohort retention, replacing manual Excel processes across three teams
- Collaborated with Product and Engineering to define and track OKRs for two platform launches, synthesizing telemetry data and user research into monthly leadership briefs
- Conducted win/loss analysis on 300+ sales opportunities, identifying pricing and positioning gaps that informed a revised sales playbook adopted company-wide

| **Independent Consultant** | Remote |
| --- | ---: |
| *Data and Strategy Consultant* | January 2020 -- August 2021 |

- Delivered analytics and market research engagements for three early-stage startups, providing actionable insights on customer segmentation, funnel performance, and unit economics
- Built financial models and investor-facing slide decks supporting two seed-round fundraises totaling $4.2M

| **Brightside Capital Group** | Chicago, IL |
| --- | ---: |
| *Business Analyst* | July 2018 -- December 2019 |

- Supported a team of six investment analysts in due diligence, financial modeling, and portfolio monitoring for a $280M mid-market private equity fund
- Built LBO and DCF models for seven deal evaluations, preparing summary memos and recommendation decks presented to the investment committee
- Automated monthly portfolio reporting using Python and Excel VBA, cutting turnaround time from three days to four hours
- Coordinated data room setup and documentation for two completed acquisitions with combined enterprise value of $95M

| **Ohio State University -- Department of Statistics** | Columbus, OH |
| --- | ---: |
| *Undergraduate Research Assistant* | January 2017 -- May 2018 |

- Assisted Professor Linda Hsu on a longitudinal study examining predictors of household financial resilience using PSID microdata across 12,000 survey respondents
- Cleaned, merged, and validated datasets in R; produced visualizations and summary tables incorporated into two conference presentations
- Co-authored a working paper submitted to the Journal of Applied Econometrics; contributed the data pipeline, robustness checks, and appendix tables

| **Nationwide Insurance** | Columbus, OH |
| --- | ---: |
| *Data Analytics Intern* | June 2017 -- August 2017 |

- Analyzed claims frequency and severity trends across four product lines using SAS and Excel, presenting findings to a team of 12 actuaries and business analysts
- Designed an interactive Excel dashboard tracking regional loss ratios that was adopted by the Property Analytics team for ongoing monthly reporting
- Completed coursework in actuarial fundamentals and presented a capstone project on predictive modeling for auto insurance pricing to senior leadership

# LEADERSHIP AND COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT

| **Stanford Consulting and Analytics Club** | Stanford, CA |
| --- | ---: |
| *President* | September 2020 -- June 2021 |

- Led a 140-member graduate organization connecting students with consulting and analytics opportunities through case workshops, speaker events, and recruiting preparation
- Organized a three-day case competition with 80 participants and five company sponsors, managing a $22,000 event budget and logistics across four venues
- Restructured the club's mentorship program, pairing 45 first-year students with industry professionals and tracking placement outcomes through a purpose-built Airtable system
- Negotiated partnerships with six companies for on-campus recruiting events, expanding the club's employer network by 40% over the prior year
- Oversaw a four-person executive board and delegated responsibilities across programming, finance, and communications committees

| **Ohio State University Alumni Association** | Remote |
| --- | ---: |
| *Career Mentor -- Volunteer* | August 2021 -- Present |

- Provide one-on-one career coaching to three to five current OSU students and recent graduates each semester, advising on resume strategy, networking, and offer negotiation
- Facilitated two virtual panels on breaking into finance and tech for a combined audience of 200 students through the College of Arts and Sciences career series
- Reviewed and gave structured written feedback on 30+ student resumes and cover letters submitted through the alumni mentoring portal
- Recognized as a top-rated mentor in the 2023 program cohort based on participant satisfaction surveys

| **Junior Achievement of Central Texas** | Austin, TX |
| --- | ---: |
| *Volunteer Financial Literacy Instructor* | September 2022 -- May 2024 |

- Delivered a six-session financial literacy curriculum to 8th graders at three Title I middle schools covering budgeting, saving, credit, and entrepreneurship
- Adapted lesson content and pacing for mixed-ability classrooms, maintaining average session engagement scores above 4.5 out of 5
- Coordinated logistics for a student business pitch competition, recruiting four local small business owners as judges and sourcing $800 in prize funding from community sponsors
- Trained two new volunteer instructors on JA curriculum standards and classroom facilitation best practices
- Reached 340 students across two academic years; 91% of post-program survey respondents reported increased confidence in managing personal finances

| **Austin Triathlon Club** | Austin, TX |
| --- | ---: |
| *Events and Logistics Coordinator* | March 2022 -- March 2023 |

- Coordinated logistics for eight club-sponsored training events and two local charity races with combined participation of 600 athletes, managing vendor relationships, course permits, and volunteer scheduling
- Built and maintained the club's event registration system in Eventbrite and managed communications to a mailing list of 850 members
- Negotiated discounted race entry and gear partnerships with four local sponsors, saving club members an estimated $3,500 annually
